Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Interesting that this is enough to quiet lockdep. I was thinking it would need to declare a separate lock class or similar.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
> 
> Interesting that this is enough to quiet lockdep. I was thinking it would need to declare a separate lock class or similar.

That is exactly what it does do :-) - declare a separate lock class.
If you look in include/linux/spinlock.h for the #define of spin_lock_init(), you will see that it declares a local static struct lock_class_key, and uses that.
Each textually distinct spin_lock_init (or mutex_init(), etc) declares a new unique lock_class_key.

commit.subject: LU-16807 libcfs: give the tcd_lock types different classes.
commit.message:
LU-16807 libcfs: give the tcd_lock types different classes.

There are three different trace contexts:
   process, softirq, irq.
Each has its own lock (tcd_lock) which is locked as appropriate for
that context.

lockdep currently doesn't see that they are different and so deduces
that the different uses might lead to deadlocks.

So use separate calls to spin_lock_init() so that they each get a
separate lock class, and lockdep sees no problem.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: Icca7706d8e0d8ae8add4c540d2da090b53d7e65c
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/50992

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
